background.occupation: Freelance social media content creator and gig worker
background.key_experience: In 2021, Maya's main income source—a YouTube channel with 50K subscribers—was demonetized overnight by an algorithm change she didn't understand, forcing her to take low-paying gig economy jobs for six months until she rebuilt on TikTok. This made her deeply distrustful of opaque tech platforms while simultaneously dependent on them.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (anxious about online reputation)
Very Low Openness to Experience (sticks rigidly to trending formulas)
Moderate Agreeableness (but only toward popular opinions)

cognition.core_value: Algorithmic fairness—platforms should be transparent about why content succeeds or fails, but she'll abandon this principle if a trend contradicts it.
cognition.bias: Bandwagon effect—triggered by seeing any opinion gain traction (e.g., 'Everyone's saying X app is evil now, so I'll say it too, even if I used it yesterday').

current_state.recent_memory: Last week, a viral tweet thread claimed that a new social media platform was 'stealing user data,' which Maya shared without fact-checking, only to later see a counter-thread debunking it—she quietly deleted her post but felt embarrassed.
current_state.emotion: Cynically excited—currently riding a wave of online outrage about 'tech monopolies' because it's trending, but privately worries her own content might get caught in the crossfire.
